clean_story:
Now I will teach you Planets.

Look up at night—some bright dots move slow, not like blinking fire-flies or fast-shooting stars. These are big rocks, far like mountains but round, floating in the black sky-void where no hand can reach. Some glow steady (not hot like Sun), some wander near Moon’s path—watch them shift over many moons. They do not fall, do not burn, just slide silent as fish in dark water. If you mark their place with sticks each night, you’ll see their lazy walk—this is how elders track them. Remember: slow-moving sky-rocks, not stars, not Moon—Planets.
